title: rerank_results
description: Run a cross-encoder reranker (default bge-reranker-v2-m3) over the candidate set returned by search_notes and narrow it to the top_k most relevant chunks for the query. Skip this call when search_notes was invoked with mode='literal'. Result shape (not modeled here for v1): an array of {chunk_id, score, text, note_path} ordered by descending rerank score, length == top_k.

query.description: The same query string passed to search_notes. The reranker scores each candidate's relevance to this query.

candidates.description: The candidate set from search_notes. Pass through the chunk_id, retrieval score, and chunk text for every candidate. Do NOT filter or reorder before reranking — the whole point is to let the cross-encoder do that.

chunk_id.description: The chunks.id from the retrieval result. Used to thread results back to the original retrieval payload after reranking.

score.description: The retrieval score from search_notes. Useful as a tiebreaker if the reranker abstains; pass the value through unchanged.

text.description: The chunk text the reranker will read. Use chunks.text_for_embed (title+heading-prefixed) for best reranker accuracy.

top_k.description: How many top results to keep after reranking. Default for v1 is 8; only override if the user asked for an unusually wide or narrow context.
